response.justification: Bogdan Szabo is an experienced full-stack and distributed-systems engineer whose career is firmly rooted in TypeScript, Node.js, and web technologies. While he has breadth across multiple domains, his C++ exposure is limited to approximately six months at National Instruments over a decade ago and a bachelor thesis project — neither of which reflects the deep, production-grade C++/STL expertise this role demands. There is no evidence of industrial automation, IEC 62443 secure-development practices, MQTT, OPC UA, or any of the industrial protocols listed as requirements or advantageous skills. The gap between his actual experience and the role's core requirements is too wide to bridge in an interview context.
strengths: Proven high-performance, distributed-systems work — handled millions of HTTP requests per second at Dunnhumby using async I/O, fibers, and low-latency data pipelines, demonstrating systems-level thinking
Event-driven architecture experience — designed and shipped an open-source, event-driven geo-data platform (GISCollective) deployed on Kubernetes, showing end-to-end architectural ownership
Strong agile and software-craftsmanship practices — consistent track record of TDD, CI/CD, trunk-based development, and mentorship across multiple employers
concerns: C++ is effectively absent from his current skill set — last substantive C++ work was ~6 months at National Instruments in 2014–2015; it does not appear in his listed skills
No industrial automation, IoT, or embedded-systems background — zero exposure to IEC 62443, IEC 61850, Modbus, OPC UA, MQTT, or any industrial protocol
Career trajectory points in the opposite direction — all recent roles (re:cinq, Optilyz, RIDE) deepen TypeScript/Node.js/cloud expertise, not C++ or industrial security
